title: Hieracium multidens
taxonomy: Hieracium multidens is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, and Hieracium multidens specifically falls under the subfamily Cichorioideae. The genus Hieracium is a group of plants commonly known as hawkweeds, which are typically characterized by their yellow or white flowers and often have a bitter or astringent taste. Within the genus Hieracium, Hieracium multidens is a specific species that is native to certain regions of the world. The classification of Hieracium multidens provides important information about its evolutionary history and its relationships to other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Hieracium multidens, also known as the many-headed hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support liver function and aid in digestion. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium multidens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
